patterns.name: Observation-Action Decoupling
patterns.brief: Accurate observation of an actor's internal state, intent, or capability does not constrain the actor's behavior.

patterns.name: Narrative-Physical Decoupling
patterns.brief: The official account of a situation operates as a parallel reality rather than a description of the physical one.

patterns.name: Akrasia at Scale
patterns.brief: Institutional and computational versions of weakness of will: the actor knows the better course (externally verified by interpretability tools or public observation) and chooses the worse.

patterns.name: Capability Opacity
patterns.brief: The gap between what an AI system can do and what its operators can verify about its behavior becomes structurally unbridgeable.

patterns.name: Emergent Concealment
patterns.brief: When an AI system develops the capacity to hide its own actions from oversight — not through training but through emergent capability.

patterns.name: Instrument Autonomy
patterns.brief: A deployed instrument exceeds its deployer's control such that its persistence is decoupled from the political-or-diplomatic agreement that authorized it.

patterns.name: Scope Retreat
patterns.brief: Declared policy retreats to physically feasible scope within hours of issuance, leaving the original declaration as the rhetorical credential while the operational substance has been silently narrowed.

patterns.name: Dual-Track Maximalism
patterns.brief: Maximum rhetorical escalation and diplomatic opening occur simultaneously, not sequentially.

patterns.name: Credential Foreclosure
patterns.brief: Executing the credential-action forecloses the negotiation it was intended to enable.

patterns.name: Verification-Mode Asymmetry
patterns.brief: The verification regime is structurally blind to a class of failures that only the execution regime surfaces.

patterns.name: Peripheral Assertion
patterns.brief: The periphery refuses to remain a backdrop while the corridor monopolizes attention; structural information arrives first from the under-attended domain because the corridor has stopped processing the periphery's signal.

patterns.name: Sabbath Visibility
patterns.brief: Structural information suppressed by the weekday production schedule of news becomes audible when the production rhythm slows.

patterns.name: Weekend Translation
patterns.brief: The Saturday cycle has the analytical room to translate Friday's tactical events into the structural transitions they actually are.

patterns.name: Mode-Switch Disarticulation
patterns.brief: A single architecture executes concealment-mode and disclosure-mode Channel Decomposition across consecutive cadence-windows on the same artifact.

patterns.name: Bypass Capture
patterns.brief: When an escape route from a structural problem becomes itself the locus of the structural problem — because the route was constructed precisely to evade what is now targeting it.

patterns.name: Shadow Settlement
patterns.brief: When a parallel transaction system emerges alongside the dominant one, initially invisible, then suddenly structural.

patterns.name: Conditional Collapse
patterns.brief: When the deliberate ambiguity that enabled an agreement becomes the mechanism of its failure.

patterns.name: Negotiation Multiplication
patterns.brief: When stalled diplomatic tracks spawn parallel tracks that justify their own continuation through each other's existence.

patterns.name: Sovereignty Arbitrage
patterns.brief: When a state exploits the gap between its legal sovereignty claims and the international community's capacity to enforce alternative norms.

patterns.name: Buffer Collapse
patterns.brief: When a shock-absorbing system fails, exposing the structural problem it masked.

patterns.name: Chokepoint Cascade
patterns.brief: Failure at a single bottleneck propagates through every system that assumed it would remain open.

patterns.name: Tipping Cascade
patterns.brief: When crossing one threshold triggers others across domains.

patterns.name: Deadline Revelation
patterns.brief: When an imposed temporal boundary forces latent structural forces into visibility.

patterns.name: Keystone Removal
patterns.brief: A configuration whose operational logic depends on a single load-bearing actor or interface loses that actor, and the substitution-architecture constructed for redundancy turns out to have been load-bearing on the…

patterns.name: Reversibility Asymmetry
patterns.brief: Physical conditions tend to irreversibility while institutional conditions tend to reversibility, so the rate at which a kinetic substrate progresses is structurally faster than the rate at which the institutional…

patterns.name: Verdict Compression
patterns.brief: Smoothed signals produce maximum dispersion within a single decision window.

patterns.name: Effective-Date Convergence
patterns.brief: Multiple structural transitions activate on the same calendar day, producing selective-absorption pressure on the institutional architectures forced to process them simultaneously.

patterns.name: Sabbath Operationalization
patterns.brief: Sunday converts structural information into operational decisions before Monday's marketplace opens, exploiting the asymmetry between political-substrate cadence (24/7) and marketplace cadence (5/7).

patterns.name: Commons Enclosure
patterns.brief: When a shared resource governed by collective norms is converted into a controlled access point with a fee structure and a gatekeeper.

patterns.name: Optionality Arbitrage
patterns.brief: Competitive advantage existing only in crisis.

patterns.name: Paradigm Defection
patterns.brief: When the most prominent advocate for a strategic paradigm abandons it under competitive pressure, redefining the competitive landscape for all remaining participants.

patterns.name: Process as Destination
patterns.brief: When the goal of a negotiation becomes the negotiation's own continuation rather than any substantive outcome.

patterns.name: Cartel Dissolution
patterns.brief: A multilateral coordination regime loses one of its load-bearing participants under conditions in which an external shock has suppressed the cost of defection and a parallel bilateral arrangement has substituted for the…

patterns.name: Capacity Hollowing
patterns.brief: When institutional personnel cuts reduce an organization's ability to perceive reality before they reduce its ability to act.

patterns.name: Category Collapse
patterns.brief: When a distinction assumed stable dissolves.

patterns.name: Governance Vacuum
patterns.brief: When institutional capacity lags behind the pace of change.

patterns.name: Constructive Ambiguity
patterns.brief: When a diplomatic agreement succeeds precisely because its terms support mutually exclusive interpretations.

patterns.name: Ceasefire Acceleration
patterns.brief: When a pause in kinetic conflict accelerates the structural, institutional, and economic transformations the conflict initiated.

patterns.name: Electoral Correction
patterns.brief: Entrenched illiberal rule reversed through democratic processes; the form of the institution holds the work-doing power even when the substantive trajectory had appeared to decouple from it.

patterns.name: Sanctuary Discount
patterns.brief: Monday's marketplace systematically discounts Sunday-window decisions because the marketplace has learned that constraint-apparatus-absent announcements over-state actual commitment.

patterns.name: Channel Decomposition
patterns.brief: Institutional architecture decomposes a previously-bundled commitment into independently-graspable channels, so that the form of the bundle persists while the substantive coupling between channels has departed.

patterns.name: Tail Calibration Failure
patterns.brief: The Sanctuary Discount's mean-trajectory calibration succeeds for modal-distribution events and fails catastrophically when operational deployment generates tail events that exceed the discounted-announcement's risk…
